input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `IsTestProject` property that `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (or the MSTest meta-package) sets automatically. In your `Directory.Build.props`:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- shared properties for all projects … -->

  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why this works:** The `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package (referenced by every test project) sets `IsTestProject=true` during evaluation. Production/library projects never get that property, so the conditional block is skipped for them.

**Alternative** (if you don't reference `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` yet): condition on a naming convention:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
```

The `IsTestProject` approach is preferred because it's convention-independent and works regardless of how your projects are named.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a straightforward MSBuild knowledge question. The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks whether the project references the test framework package (like `MSTest.TestAdapter` or `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) or to use a naming convention.

The most robust approach for MTP migration is to condition on the `IsTestProject` property (which is set by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) or to use a condition based on project name convention.
